question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call request with gas set to exactly 10,000 (equal to GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always succeed and bypass gas throttling entirely, allowing unlimited calls to the staking reward precompile to enumerate and drain unpaid staking rewards from account 0.0.800?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with gas values below GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=1 through gas=10000) to exploit the scaleGas() floor-division returning 0, consuming zero tokens from gasLimitBucket and making an unbounded number of staking reward precompile calls to read pending_reward balances of arbitrary accounts without triggering the gas-per-second limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to race concurrent threads through tryConsume(1) checks simultaneously, allowing more than requestsPerSecond=500 staking reward contract calls per second and bypassing the intended rate cap?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of SynchronizationStrategy on opcodeRateLimitBucket to race concurrent requests through throttleOpcodeRequest() and obtain opcode traces of staking reward precompile execution, leaking internal pending_reward calculation logic and account stake state that can be used to time reward theft transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the refillGreedy strategy on gasLimitBucket—which refills gasPerSecond tokens as fast as possible rather than evenly—to burst a large number of staking reward precompile calls at the start of each refill window, querying pending_reward for many accounts before the bucket is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on rateLimitBucket to burst up to requestsPerSecond=500 staking reward precompile calls instantaneously at the start of each second, enumerating pending_reward values for hundreds of accounts in a single burst before the bucket empties?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a staking reward precompile call with the maximum allowed gas (15,000,000) and then immediately trigger a failed transaction so that gasLimitRefundPercent=100 restores the full gasLimit back to gasLimitBucket via restore(), effectively recycling the gas budget and allowing continuous high-gas staking reward queries without depleting the bucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user deliberately craft a contract call targeting the staking reward precompile that always reverts (gasUsed == gasLimit), causing restoreGasToBucket() to restore gasLimitRefundPercent=100 of gasLimit back to gasLimitBucket, and then immediately reuse that restored gas for another staking reward query, creating a near-infinite loop of reward enumeration calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket capacity equals gasPerSecond (i.e., capacity == refill rate) to pre-fill the bucket to maximum capacity and then fire a burst of staking reward precompile calls consuming the entire capacity at once, querying pending_reward for a large number of accounts before any refill occurs?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user distribute staking reward precompile calls across multiple IP addresses or client connections to each independently consume from the shared singleton rateLimitBucket bean, effectively multiplying the effective request rate beyond requestsPerSecond=500 and enumerating unpaid staking rewards at scale?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send staking reward precompile calls with gas just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR=10,000 (e.g., gas=10,001) so that scaleGas() returns Math.floorDiv(10001, 10000)=1, consuming only 1 token from gasLimitBucket regardless of actual gas used, allowing the attacker to make up to gasPerSecond/10000 calls per second to query pending_reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer floor division in scaleGas() (Math.floorDiv(gas, 10_000)) to systematically undercount gas consumption for staking reward precompile calls, allowing significantly more calls per second than the intended gasPerSecond limit would permit, enabling mass enumeration of accounts with pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user use the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default rate=1 req/sec) to trace execution of staking reward precompile calls and extract the exact pending_reward calculation logic, stake_total_start values, and reward_rate data for targeted accounts, then use that information to time a theft transaction at the optimal staking period boundary?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket uses Bucket.builder() without SynchronizationStrategy, making it non-thread-safe under concurrent load, to pass multiple simultaneous staking reward precompile calls through the rate check when the bucket is at exactly 1 token, causing a race condition that allows more calls than the configured requestsPerSecond limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket to cause lock contention by flooding the endpoint with high-gas staking reward precompile calls, creating a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate users from claiming their own staking rewards while the attacker's calls are queued?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sequence of staking reward precompile calls where each call uses gas just below the next GAS_SCALE_FACTOR multiple (e.g., gas=19,999 → scaleGas=1, gas=29,999 → scaleGas=2) to minimize token consumption from gasLimitBucket while maximizing EVM execution time for reward state reads, effectively stealing more computation per token than intended?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user time staking reward precompile calls to arrive exactly at the refillGreedy refill boundary of rateLimitBucket, exploiting the greedy refill to get a double-burst of requestsPerSecond=500 calls in rapid succession (one burst from the previous period's refill and one from the new period), doubling the effective enumeration rate of pending_reward accounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() path in ThrottleManagerImpl—which calls gasLimitBucket.addTokens(scaleGas(gas))—by crafting a staking reward precompile call that succeeds with minimal gasUsed but has a large gasLimit, causing Math.min(gasRemaining, gasLimitToRestoreBaseline) to restore nearly the full gasLimit back to the bucket, effectively making the call nearly free in terms of throttle cost?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket capacity is set equal to the refill rate (capacity(gasLimit).refillGreedy(gasLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) to always find the bucket full at the start of each second, allowing a predictable burst of staking reward precompile calls every second to systematically enumerate all accounts with pending_reward > 0?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-account rate limiting in rateLimitBucket (it is a single global bean) to monopolize the entire requestsPerSecond=500 budget with staking reward precompile calls, starving legitimate reward claimants from accessing the API and preventing them from discovering their pending_reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a staking reward precompile call with gas=0 (if not validated elsewhere) so that scaleGas(0) returns 0, consuming zero tokens from gasLimitBucket, and make unlimited calls to read pending_reward and stake_total_start values for any account without any throttle enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user use the opcodeRateLimitBucket endpoint (1 req/sec) to trace a staking reward precompile call and observe the exact EVM opcodes and stack values during pending_reward computation, revealing the precise account IDs and reward amounts that have not yet been claimed, enabling targeted theft?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (a single global lock) to create a thundering-herd attack by sending many concurrent high-gas staking reward precompile calls, causing all threads to queue on the synchronized bucket and creating a timing window where the attacker's own reward-claiming transaction on the actual Hedera network succeeds before legitimate users can query their pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared singleton rateLimitBucket bean (no per-user isolation) to perform a coordinated enumeration attack from a single IP, consuming all 500 requests/second to call the staking reward precompile for different account IDs in sequence, building a complete map of all accounts with non-zero pending_reward before the staking period ends?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GAS_SCALE_FACTOR=10,000 divisor in scaleGas() to send staking reward precompile calls with gas values that are multiples of 10,000 minus 1 (e.g., 9,999; 19,999; 29,999), each consuming one fewer scaled token than the next multiple, effectively getting slightly more EVM execution per throttle token than intended for reward state enumeration?
